title: forsteronia corymbosa
intro: Forsteronia corymbosa, also known as the Guianese snail vine, is a type of climbing plant native to the Guiana Shield in South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Forsteronia corymbosa include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in the removal of intestinal parasites. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine practices.
